title: GsdStateContracts
description: COMP-GSD-6 run-state artifacts. `state` is the continuously-flushed checkpoint at .compose/gsd/<feature>/state.json — written pre-plan (phase:planning, flowId:null), updated with flowId after stratum.plan, heartbeat-bumped per task event, marked resumeReady once decomposedTasks is populated, and terminally flushed (complete|stuck|budget|failed). A hard crash leaves status:running + a dead pid, which readers derive as 'crashed'. `query` is the read-only envelope emitted by `compose gsd query` — it adds the reader-only derived statuses 'crashed' and 'absent' to the runner taxonomy, plus the advisory heartbeatStale flag. decomposedTasks stores FULL enriched task objects (id/depends_on/description), not ids, so crash-recovery can synthesize a resume graph (it reuses the gsd-stuck.json#/definitions/pause decomposedTasks shape).

state.title: GsdRunState
state.description: The continuously-flushed run checkpoint. Status is what the RUNNER writes; readers derive 'crashed'/'absent' from this via deriveRunStatus (lib/gsd-state.js).

feature.description: Feature code the gsd run is building (e.g. COMP-GSD-6).

flowId.description: Stratum flow id. null in the pre-plan 'planning' checkpoint; set after stratum.plan returns.

pid.description: OS pid of the running process. The authoritative crash signal: status:running + a dead pid (process.kill(pid,0) ESRCH) ⇒ crashed.

phase.description: Coarse lifecycle phase. 'planning' is the pre-plan checkpoint (before stratum.plan); the failed-vs-fatal boundary is whether this checkpoint exists.

status.description: Runner-written status. 'failed' = an orderly exception AFTER the planning checkpoint (the dispatch-try catch). 'running' persisted with a dead pid is derived as 'crashed' by readers (never self-written).

heartbeatAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp bumped on every flush AND from the per-task push-event path. A stale heartbeat on a LIVE pid is advisory only (heartbeatStale) — never a crash verdict.

headless.description: True if launched under the --headless supervisor.

resumeReady.description: True once decomposedTasks is flushed post-decompose. Gates the supervisor crash branch: --resume when true, fresh restart when false (crashed during plan/decompose — nothing merged yet).

decomposedTasks.description: Full enriched task objects (id/depends_on/description, NOT bare ids), so the crash bridge can synthesize a resume graph. Empty until decompose completes.

completedTaskIds.description: Task ids whose validated result is already merged/in the blackboard. Resume skips these.

lastTaskId.description: Most recently completed task id (progress hint).

budget.description: Optional mirror of the live flow budget {caps,consumed} for query burn reporting.

query.title: GsdQuerySnapshot
query.description: Read-only envelope emitted by `compose gsd query <feature>`. Synthesized with a fixed source precedence (state.json → pause.json → budget.json → absent). One status vocabulary across query/supervisor/tests.

status.description: Derived status. 'crashed' (running+dead-pid) and 'absent' (no run state anywhere) are reader-only; the rest mirror the runner taxonomy.

heartbeatStale.description: Advisory: status is 'running' on a live pid but the heartbeat exceeded the stale threshold (run may be wedged). Never implies crashed.

resumeReady.description: Whether a crash here would --resume (true) or restart fresh (false).

pause.description: When paused: the pause kind + detail (from pause.json).

budget.description: Cumulative/flow budget info when available (from state.json or budget.json).
